messages.subject: [R-jobs] Individual Contributors and Team Leaders positions in Statistics at Johnson & Johnson
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Individual Contributors and Team Leaders positions in Statistics at Johnson & Johnson
messages.body_plain:
The Statistics & Decision Sciences community at Johnson & Johnson is searching for individuals to help in the mission of discovering, developing, manufacturing, and delivering beneficial medicines for patients. The group resides within the Janssen R&D organization in Johnson & Johnson's pharmaceutical segment. We need individuals with skills and motivation to consistently and adaptively lead, connect, shape, and deliver. There will be endless opportunities to successfully contribute to the mission and to grow personally. 

These positions range from entry to senior levels: Positions are in the following worldwide locations: Raritan, NJ, Titusville, NJ,  Spring House, PA, and  La Jolla, CA in the USA; Beerse, Belgium, and Leiden, The Netherlands, in the EU; and Shanghai, China.  

If you are interested, please see a detailed description and a path to apply below. Thank you for your consideration, and please feel free to forward this to any professionals in your network you feel may be a good match. 

Sincerely,
Bill Pikounis on behalf of the Statistics & Decision Sciences team at Johnson & Johnson

Current open positions can be found at http://careers.jnj.com/   by entering keyword "Statistics & Decision Sciences" in the search box on the page.

#########
More Details:

Job Class Description
---------------------------
Janssen Research & Development, L.L.C, a member of Johnson & Johnson's Family of Companies is recruiting for a number of Statistics & Decision Sciences positions.  These positions range from entry to senior levels: Positions are in the following worldwide locations Raritan, NJ, Titusville, NJ,  Spring House, PA, and  La Jolla, CA in the USA; Beerse, Belgium, and Leiden, The Netherlands, in the EU; and Shanghai, China. 
  
Janssen Research & Development, LLC discovers and develops innovative medical solutions to address important unmet medical needs in oncology, immunology, neuroscience, infectious diseases and vaccines, and cardiovascular and metabolic diseases. Please visit http://www.JanssenRnD.com  for more information.  

Statistics & Decision Sciences integrates broad and deep expertise across Janssen Research & Development in areas that range from Drug Discovery through Phases 2 and 3 clinical studies and post-market Medical Affairs and Real World Evidence. As part of Quantitative Sciences that comprises Statistics & Decision Sciences and Clinical Pharmacology & Pharmacometrics, we work in cross-disciplinary teams and apply quantitative principles across the R&D portfolio to enable optimal decision-making at the earliest time point and in the most efficient manner. 

Primary responsibilities include collaborating with researchers on study design, data analysis, interpretation, reporting and communication of data evidence for research, development, and/or marketed product needs in the Janssen family of companies. Appropriate research and application of statistical methodology is essential. Co-authorship of publications and presentations is also expected.  The successful candidate must be constantly eager to expand their statistical, communications, and computing expertise. The candidate must also enjoy the process of building long-term collaborative relationships, be at ease with either a non-rigid or rigid structure to projects, and be organized in handling numerous projects simultaneously. Participation in the external professional community is expected as well.

Qualifications
----------------
A Master's degree or PhD in Statistics or related Quantitative Sciences field and a minimum of experience is required dependent on the level of position. Proficiency of R and SAS statistical software is required.  Demonstrated strong written, oral, and interpersonal communication skills are required. Knowledge of linear mixed models and multivariate analysis is required. Familiarity with modern design and data analysis techniques such as adaptive designs, modeling and simulation, data visualization, robust statistics and analysis of high-dimensional data is preferred.

Current open positions can be found at http://careers.jnj.com/  with keyword "Statistics & Decision Sciences".

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Quantitative Risk and Decision Analysis Consultant
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Quantitative Risk and Decision Analysis Consultant
messages.body_plain:
Quantitative Risk and Decision Analysis Consultant

 

Deadline for applications: May 31, 2016

Location: Boulder, CO

 

We are currently looking for a motivated and talented senior consultant in
Quantitative Risk and Decision Analysis who desires to use quantitative
skills to make a difference in real-life problems. 

 

Responsibilities:

-          Framing and scoping of projects, and proposing of appropriate
analytical approaches

-          Quantitative model development, review and interpretation

-          Facilitation of meetings and presentations to clients, both in
oral and written form

-          Ability to work independently with little direct supervision, as
well as collaborate in teams of consultants within EpiX and with clients

-          Generation of business leads from existing and new clients

 

Requirements:

-          PhD in operations research, applied statistics or equivalent
quantitative field. Applicants with a MSc. degree but with exceptional
proven equivalent expertise might be considered

-          At least 4 years of working experience in quantitative modeling

-          Capable of creative and applied thinking

-          Excellent and demonstrable communication skills, both oral and in
written

-          Ability to deliver on time and under tight deadlines

-          Provide proof of work eligibility in the United States

 

Desired qualifications:

-          Knowledge and experience of advanced optimization techniques,
including stochastic optimization

-          Demonstrable experience using decision-analysis approaches and
applying these techniques in real-life decision-problems

-          Experience with Monte Carlo simulation and/or other simulation
modeling methods

-          Advanced working knowledge of the R statistical language, Excel's
VBA and/or other statistical programming environments

-          Experience with Monte Carlo simulation Add-ins to Excel (e.g.
@RISK, Crystal Ball, RiskSolver)

-          Experience working in a private/consulting environment

-          Experience in business development

 

What we offer?

-          Challenging engagements while working with a diverse, informal
and collaborative team

-          Frequent direct contact with clients, and opportunities to lead
client engagements

-          Good work-life balance in beautiful Colorado

-          Competitive salary

-          Paid vacation, 401(K), paid sick-time and paid professional
development

 

Founded in 2003, EpiX Analytics provides pragmatic answers to decision
makers using sound quantitative methods. We are looking for professionals
who are passionate about being on the cutting edge of quantitative modeling
and analytical methods and would enjoy helping a diverse portfolio of
private and public sector clients and work.

 

If you are interested in joining our team, please send a resume and cover
letter to careers at epixanalytics.com. 

 

Successful applicant(s) will be required to pass a background check.

 


	
Barbara S. O'Neill 

Office Manager

Phone: +1 303 440 8524 Fax: +1 303 440 0320 

 <mailto:fzagmutt at epixanalytics.com> boneill at epixanalytics.com |
<http://www.epixanalytics.com/> www.epixanalytics.com 

 

 

 


	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Data Scientist/ R Software Developer at Tessella in Needham, MA
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Data Scientist/ R Software Developer at Tessella in Needham, MA
messages.body_plain:
Hello,
This is a job located at another division in Altran I wanted to share.

We are seeking talented Data Scientists with a passion for R, software development and analytical skills and a strong academic and/or commercial background.

Due to our continued growth and success, we have a number of open positions available. Even if you have not yet graduated (but are graduating in the near future), we welcome hearing from you

We can offer relocation assistance to the Massachusetts area.

Apply by going here:
https://careers-foliage.icims.com/jobs/1457/data-scientist---software-developer/job

Tessella, Part of the Altran Group,  is a global analytics, software services and IT consulting company that works with some of the world's leading organizations in pharmaceuticals, energy, consumer goods and the public sector. We use our unique blend of science, engineering and sector expertise to deliver innovative solutions to complex real-world challenges. As well as being acclaimed custom software producers, we are experts in the fields of big data and analytics, and our pharmaceutical clients rely on us to help them draw meaningful conclusions from the flood of clinical data.

Thanks so much,
Katie McAuliffe | Lead Talent Acquisition Partner
20 North Avenue, Burlington, MA 01803
P: 781.993.5546
Foliage, part of the Altran Group

________________________________

This message is intended only for the designated recipie...{{dropped:11}}
